refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding 'documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs' and 'history of baselines/calibration updates.' High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance targets are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, operator ID, and mission context; (c) all updates are logged with before/after values and justification; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline record is complete and auditable.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets from worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle time, energy budget, availability SLA).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance targets as a baseline with full context (timestamp, operator, mission ID, environmental conditions).
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement and calibration updates with audit trail (who, when, why, before/after values).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Ensure traceability from worksite inputs through derived requirements to recorded baselines and all downstream allocations.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests worksite mission parameters (via Tasking interface), applies deterministic algorithms to compute performance targets, records baselines in a versioned database, and exposes a query/audit API.

DP-B: Distributed Derivation with Local Caching – Performance derivation logic embedded in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, with periodic synchronization to a central audit log; reduces latency but increases coupling and diffusion risk.

DP-C: Manual Baseline Entry with Post-Hoc Validation – Operators manually enter performance targets via a UI form; system validates against mission context and records history; lower automation, higher human error risk.

DP-D: Hybrid Adaptive Derivation – Automatic derivation with operator override capability; system learns from historical mission outcomes to refine future target estimates; adds complexity but improves long-term accuracy.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests worksite mission parameters (via Tasking interface), applies deterministic algorithms to compute performance targets, records baselines in a versioned database, and exposes a query/audit API for traceability and compliance.

potential_sources: Dependency on Tasking interface for mission context input – well-defined, external contract.
Dependency on persistent storage (database) for baseline and history – standard architectural pattern, low coupling risk.
Potential coupling to Safety Services if safety margin derivation requires real-time safety constraints – mitigated by explicit interface contract (SafetyAlert).
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling because it is a pure computation + storage module with well-defined input/output contracts. No circular dependencies or hidden state sharing detected. Deterministic algorithms reduce runtime coupling variability.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context is always available via Tasking interface at the time of performance derivation. Risk: If mission context is incomplete or delayed, derivation may fail or produce invalid targets. Mitigation: Implement validation and fallback to cached baseline.

Assumption: Performance derivation algorithms are stable and validated offline. Risk: Incorrect algorithm logic could propagate to all downstream allocations. Mitigation: Require algorithm validation and traceability to engineering standards.

Assumption: Database is reliable and audit trail is tamper-proof. Risk: Loss of history or audit trail corruption could violate compliance requirements. Mitigation: Implement database redundancy, checksums, and immutable audit log.

Assumption: Safety margin derivation is deterministic and does not require real-time feedback from Safety Services. Risk: If safety constraints change dynamically, derived targets may become unsafe. Mitigation: Implement periodic re-validation against current safety constraints.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound. Recommend adding explicit specification of: (1) algorithm validation and traceability requirements, (2) database schema and audit log format, (3) fallback behavior if mission context is unavailable, (4) interface contract for Tasking input (required fields, data types, validation rules).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary that integrates all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and provides the unified performance derivation, baseline management, and audit capability. The Performance Derivation Engine is a logical component within NGHE Solution that owns the derivation algorithms, baseline database, and audit API.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite mission context (terrain type, payload mass, cycle time target, energy budget, availability SLA, environmental conditions) from Site Manager or Tele-operator. Used as input to performance derivation algorithms.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs derived performance targets, baseline records, and audit trail data to monitoring and compliance systems. Enables Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality to query and validate performance baselines.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints and alerts from Safety Services; sends derived safety margins and performance targets that may affect safety envelope. Ensures performance derivation respects current safety constraints.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for distributed access to baseline records and audit logs. Enables remote Site Managers and Maintenance teams to query performance history and validate compliance.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of performance derivation and baseline management. All required interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are already exposed at this level. Minimal diffusion risk: the Performance Derivation Engine is a cohesive logical component with clear ownership and no need to scatter logic across Vehicle or Teleoperation.

Vehicle module is primarily responsible for physical actuation and real-time control. Placing performance derivation here would scatter system-level logic into a subsystem, creating tight coupling between mission-level requirements and vehicle-specific implementation. High diffusion risk: Vehicle would need to expose Tasking and audit interfaces, duplicating system-level concerns.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operator interface and command relay. Performance derivation is not a teleoperation concern. Placing it here would conflate operator interface logic with system-level requirement management, creating high diffusion and coupling risk. Teleoperation should consume derived targets, not produce them.

Safety Services owns safety constraint validation and alert generation. Performance derivation includes safety margin computation, which is related but distinct. Placing full derivation in Safety Services would overload that module and create tight coupling between performance and safety logic. Better to keep them separate with explicit SafetyAlert interface contract.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Performance Derivation Engine (as a logical component within NGHE Solution) is the correct architectural home because: (1) it is a system-level concern that integrates mission context, vehicle capabilities, and safety constraints; (2) it owns the baseline database and audit trail, which are system-level assets; (3) all required interfaces are already exposed at NGHE Solution level; (4) containment here minimizes diffusion and coupling to subsystems.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution becomes a 'god module' with too many responsibilities, performance derivation could be buried and hard to maintain. Mitigation: Establish clear logical component boundaries within NGHE Solution (e.g., Performance Derivation Engine, Baseline Manager, Audit Logger) with explicit internal interfaces.

Risk: If performance derivation algorithms are tightly coupled to specific vehicle or teleoperation implementations, future system variants may require significant rework. Mitigation: Design algorithms to be vehicle-agnostic, parameterized by vehicle capabilities (max speed, energy capacity, etc.) passed via Tasking interface.
Risk: If audit trail grows unbounded, database performance may degrade. Mitigation: Implement retention policies, archival strategies, and query optimization.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign Performance Derivation Engine to NGHE Solution. Recommend further decomposition at next level to define: (1) Baseline Manager (owns baseline database and versioning), (2) Derivation Algorithm Module (owns computation logic), (3) Audit Logger (owns immutable audit trail). Each should have explicit internal interfaces and clear ownership.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) automatic derivation from mission context via Tasking input, (2) baseline recording with full context, (3) managed history with audit trail, (4) traceability via query API. DP-A is deterministic, auditable, and supports high safety criticality. No functional gaps detected.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk: DP-A is assigned to NGHE Solution, which is the natural system-level owner. No logic is scattered across Vehicle or Teleoperation.
Interface contracts are well-defined (Tasking in, Telemetry/SafetyAlert/Network Link out). No hidden dependencies or implicit couplings.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution containment score is 95/100. The module boundary is clear: Performance Derivation Engine is a cohesive logical component with single responsibility (derive, record, manage performance targets). Ownership is unambiguous. Recommended to further decompose at next level into Baseline Manager, Derivation Algorithm, and Audit Logger sub-components.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible: (1) Derive performance targets from test mission contexts and validate against expected outputs. (2) Record baselines and confirm all metadata (timestamp, operator, mission ID) is captured. (3) Perform update operations and verify audit trail is complete and immutable. (4) Trace from worksite input through derived requirement to baseline record. All verification activities are deterministic and repeatable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: CR-NGHE-005 is allocated to NGHE Solution (Performance Derivation Engine). Sub-FRs are allocated to logical components within NGHE Solution (Baseline Manager, Derivation Algorithm, Audit Logger). Interfaces are explicit (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link). Ownership is unambiguous.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-A specification to include: (a) algorithm validation and traceability requirements, (b) database schema and audit log format, (c) fallback behavior if mission context is unavailable, (d) interface contract for Tasking input (required fields, data types, validation rules).
Define internal logical component boundaries within NGHE Solution: Baseline Manager, Derivation Algorithm Module, Audit Logger. Specify internal interfaces and data flow.
Establish performance and reliability targets for the Performance Derivation Engine (e.g., derivation latency < 100ms, audit trail availability > 99.9%, database consistency guarantees).
Define algorithm validation and certification process to ensure safety criticality is met.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 is not atomic. The refined FR has four sub-FRs that require further decomposition: (1) derive performance targets from mission context, (2) record baselines with full context, (3) maintain managed history with audit trail, (4) ensure traceability. Each sub-FR has distinct design concerns and should be decomposed to the next level. DP-A and IM assignment (NGHE Solution) are sound and pass all gates, but the functional hierarchy is incomplete. Recommend proceeding to next decomposition level for each sub-FR.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR explicitly identifies four sub-FRs that are logically distinct and require separate design solutions. Sub-FR 1 (derive targets) requires algorithm design and mission context parsing. Sub-FR 2 (record baselines) requires database schema and metadata capture. Sub-FR 3 (maintain history) requires versioning and audit log design. Sub-FR 4 (ensure traceability) requires query API and traceability matrix design. Each sub-FR should be decomposed to identify candidate DPs and IMs at the next level.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets from worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle time, energy budget, availability SLA).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance targets as a baseline with full context (timestamp, operator, mission ID, environmental conditions).
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement and calibration updates with audit trail (who, when, why, before/after values).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Ensure traceability from worksite inputs through derived requirements to recorded baselines and all downstream allocations.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is always available via Tasking interface at the time of performance derivation.
Performance derivation algorithms are stable and validated offline.
Database is reliable and audit trail is tamper-proof.
Safety margin derivation is deterministic and does not require real-time feedback from Safety Services.
NGHE Solution module can accommodate Performance Derivation Engine as a logical component without becoming a 'god module'.
open_risks: If mission context is incomplete or delayed, derivation may fail or produce invalid targets.
Incorrect algorithm logic could propagate to all downstream allocations.
Loss of history or audit trail corruption could violate compliance requirements.
If safety constraints change dynamically, derived targets may become unsafe.
Performance Derivation Engine could become tightly coupled to specific vehicle or teleoperation implementations, limiting system variant flexibility.
evidence_needed: Algorithm validation and traceability documentation (e.g., requirements traceability matrix linking algorithms to engineering standards).
Database schema and audit log format specification.
Fallback behavior specification for missing or delayed mission context.
Interface contract for Tasking input (required fields, data types, validation rules).
Performance and reliability targets for Performance Derivation Engine (latency, availability, consistency).
Verification plan for each sub-FR (test cases, acceptance criteria, traceability).
Safety criticality analysis and certification evidence.
